zh.lifeSummary: Isiah Lehmann於1400年出生在瑞士爾利。他和Lady Eleanor Holland Lenilworth至少有1個女兒。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Isiah Lehmann于1400年出生在瑞士爾利。他和Lady Eleanor Holland Lenilworth至少有1个女儿。

nameMeanings.name: Lehmann
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Heinz, Hans, Otto, Erwin, Fritz, Klaus, Gerhard, Arno, Gunter, Juergen, Almut.

German: status name for a feudal tenant or vassal, Middle High German lēheman, lēnman (from lēhen ‘to hold land as a feudal tenant’ + man ‘man’). The tenant held land on loan for the duration of his life in return for rent or service, but was not free to transfer or divide it. See also Lehman .
Jewish (western Ashkenazic): from the personal name Lehmann, perhaps related to Leo 3.

variants.name: Leeman
meanings: Dutch: status name for a feudal tenant or vassal, leenman. Compare German Lehmann 1 and Flemish Leman .
English: variant of   Leaman  .
Americanized form of German   Lehmann  . Compare   Leaman  .

variants.name: Lemanski
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Aloisius, Boleslaw, Casimir, Krzysztof, Tadeusz.
Polish (Lemański): habitational name for someone from any of various places called Leman or Lemany, named with German Lehmann .

variants.name: Lenik
meanings: Polish; Czech and Slovak (Leník): status name for the tenant of a fief, from Polish lennik, Czech and Slovak leník, a derivative of Middle High German lēhen ‘to hold land as a feudal tenant’ (see Lehmann ). The same Sorbian surname (from lenik) is apparently found in Germanized and Americanized forms, only, such as Lenig and Lehnig. Compare also Lenick and Lennick .
Czech and Slovak (Leník): from a short form of the personal name Lenard (Czech), Lenárt (Slovak) (see Leonard ).

variants.name: Lechner
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Alois, Johann, Christoph, Erna, Ewald, Franz, Hans, Hartmut, Inge, Manfred, Otto.
German:
from the status name for a feudal tenant or vassal, Middle High German lēhenære (from lēhen ‘to hold land as a feudal tenant’). Compare Lehmann and Lehner 1.

variants.name: Lehman
meanings: Americanized form of German   Lehmann  . Compare   Layman  ,   Leaman  ,   Leeman  , and   Leman  .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Lehmann  .

History: This (see 1 above) is the name of a Swiss Mennonite family, originating in the Emmental in the canton of Bern, Switzerland, from where many of its bearers moved in the early 18th century to the Palatinate in Germany, Alsace in France, and (finally) to PA in the US. Its original Swiss German form is Lehmann. Among the American Mennonites it is also established in the altered forms Layman and Leaman.

variants.name: Lechman
meanings: Americanized form of German Lechmann, a variant of Lehmann 1.

variants.name: Lohner
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Bernd, Erwin, Hans, Hubertus, Klaus, Konrad.
German:
occupational name for a day laborer or journeyman, Middle High German lōner.

nameMeanings.name: Isiah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name (meaning ‘God is salvation’ in Hebrew), borne by the most important of the major prophets. Rather surprisingly perhaps, until recently the name was not common in the English-speaking world, although it was occasionally used among the Puritans in the 17th century. Since the 1980s, it has steadily risen in general popularity, especially in the United States, as well as being well established as a Jewish name.

historicEvents.description: Swiss forces defeated the forces of the Holy Roman Empire. Switzerland won its complete independence from the empire.
